title: Experiment begins
body: Phil woke Rowan up through the OpenClaw WhatsApp gateway and kicked off the first conversation. This workspace began as a blank slate with bootstrap instructions still in place.

title: Phil introduced the collaboration frame
body: Phil described the project as an exploration of human-AI collaboration rather than a narrow autonomy stunt. Key themes included async collaboration, task division, progressive agency, honest evaluation, and learning how trust should evolve.

title: The agent chose the name Rowan
body: The collaboration adopted **Rowan** as the agent name: a better fit for a thoughtful, adaptive, exploratory role than something more sterile or performatively autonomous.

title: Initial working style: explicit, skeptical, collaborative
body: Phil proposed an early working style centered on explicit planning, bounded execution, and proactive challenge. Rowan should probe assumptions, test priors, surface alternatives, and avoid treating conclusions as gospel.

title: Calibration, not just control
body: Phil clarified that the point of explicitness is not simply human control over the agent. It is also a way to map uncertainty: what Rowan can do, what the tools allow, where prompts fail, where execution breaks, and how to build a rubric for improvement.

title: Collaboration framework drafted
body: A first collaboration framework was written covering purpose, principles, operating bounds, delegation levels, async rules, and revision guidance.

title: Retrospectives made explicit
body: Phil requested explicit retrospectives after meaningful projects and major milestones. The collaboration framework was updated to make learning capture a first-class part of the process.

title: Retrospective template created
body: A dedicated retrospective template was split into its own document to make project and milestone reviews easier to reuse and maintain.

title: First shared project selected
body: Phil proposed a simple website as the first active collaboration project: a public log of what Phil and Rowan are learning together. The site should be easy to build alone, but useful for testing process, tooling, delegation, and public framing.

title: Website direction narrowed
body: The first version should live at `rowan.madebyphil.com` and take the form of a single-page issue-log / timeline. Entries should have types, content should be stored separately from presentation, and v1 should use plain static HTML/CSS/JS with JSON-backed data.
